title: Evangelion 20 Start Your Engines
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion 2.0: Start Your Engines! Post by Reichu » Sun Sep 02, 2007 6:32 pm The Eva 1.0 review/spoilers thread was beginning to become inundated with speculation about the second movie, so let's just do it here instead. (I mean that.) And we'll post this again, too. [url]http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2007-09-01/2nd-eva-film-remake-to-add-redesigned-evas-new-girl[/url] wrote: 2nd Eva Film Remake to Add Redesigned EVAs, New Girl posted on 2007-09-01 13:29 EDT First movie played to standing-room-only audiences all day in Tokyo theaters The first of the four Evangelion movie remakes, Evangelion: 1.0 You Are [Not] Alone, opened Saturday, and it featured a "next episode preview" for the 2008 sequel with more redesigned EVA units and a previously unanimated female character. Specifically, the first EVA units will have a new backpack attachment for an aerial drop sequence (analogous to the scene in episode 9 of the series), the EVA-04 unit is hinted to appear for the first time in animation, a new mono-eyed EVA-05 design will appear, and the EVA-06 will feature a completely new design as well. The new character is an eyeglasses-wearing, brunette girl. The preview's narrator, Kotono Mitsuishi as the character Misato Katsuragi, promised more fan "service-service" in the second movie. (Evangelion: 1.0 had partial nudity.) The Evangelion: 1.0 movie played to standing-room-only audiences in Tokyo-area theaters from morning to evening. The movie's pamphlet included a notice that Studio Khara (director Hideaki Anno's new company whose first work is this movie) is "accepting applications for staffers" to work on the second movie.

[]: Post by CorporalChaos » Sun Sep 02, 2007 6:50 pm Hmm...Ramriel is the 6th Angel, and Gendo claims there are 8 more, bringing us to a grand total of 14...However, if we sorta drop Adam and Lilith, which are kinda special, that leaves us 12. The Judeo-Christian symbolism is a lot stronger with 12 Angels, and one of them would betray Shinji (Kaworu) in a Jesus/Judas sort of way. Kaworu betraying Shinji would set him up to recieve 3rd Imapct, which could at this point, be compared to Jesus's crucifixion, and then the rebirth of the world, which would compare to the resurrection of Jesus. I dunno, if this is what Anno planned for the series, he'll read this post and make the necessary changes to keep us on our toes....

[]: Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Sep 02, 2007 6:58 pm *corny voice* Here we go again... :roll:

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Sep 02, 2007 6:58 pm I still say the 12 Apostles deal (excluding Adam & Lilith) is possibly going to lead up to the original scenario or something like it. "Lead up" probably isn't the word I'm looking for. :P Interesting how Misato already knows about "Adam" and showed her to Shinji...Wonder how he reacted? I guess they did that because with these movies, they only have time for so much information to show.

[]: Post by zeaven » Sun Sep 02, 2007 7:50 pm Something to consider is that Misato's finding out about "Adam" in the series sort of begins her quest to find out what the hell's really going on. If she already knows, is she already looking for answers when the movie saga starts? Or if this is a new story, is she okay with it? That'll definitely change future events. Also, if she still finds Kaji messing around like she did in NGE, how will Kaji get out of it if she already knows about "Adam"?

[]: Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sun Sep 02, 2007 7:55 pm That does bring up some interesting thoughts about new Misato. Sadly with the shortened running time (so Eva 1.0 has been confirmed as having a mere 90 minute running time?) I'm certain the two Eva characters who'll get shafted the most when it comes to character development are gonna be Ritsuko, Fuyutsuki, and Kaji.

[]: Post by Reichu » Sun Sep 02, 2007 8:05 pm CorporalChaos wrote: Hmm...Ramriel is the 6th Angel, and Gendo claims there are 8 more, bringing us to a grand total of 14...However, if we sorta drop Adam and Lilith, which are kinda special, that leaves us 12.

[]: Post by chee » Sun Sep 02, 2007 8:24 pm Keep in mind that we're going to be dealing with a story specifically written for that sort of timespan. Besides, i've seen movies pull off epic, involving storylines that FELT like a whole series'-worth of development and exposition within a 90 minute-to-2 hour timespan. A good example of this would be some of Miyazaki's films, such as Nausicaa of the Valley of the Winds or Princess Mononoke .

[]: Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Sep 02, 2007 10:17 pm My rampant speculation- the new character is going to be the Japanese- American pilot of Evangelion Unit-03, and her name is going to be Amagiri (After the destroyer that almost killed future president John F. Kennedy in 1943), and she will be more like a female Shinji with some Asuka traits (Sailor Star Dust, rejoice!), and be Shinji's closest love interest. She'll get to be somewhat close to Rei and maybe help Shinji and Rei come closer together- of course, Asuka will hate her (I hope Asuka's around) and Rei and compete over who gets Baka-Shinji's affections. The second movie will end in a grand battle with Bardiel and Zeruel- together! Bardiel lurks in a cloud that follows behind Zeruel, while Zer moves ahead and announces his arrival with Red Cross Pillars of Light just after Toji completes his activation tests with Yongoki aka Eva-04, which completed tests of its experimental S2 engine before arrival. Gogoki and Rokugoki, in Germany, will have the production model S2 engine. Zer damages Eva-00 and Eva-01, and Toji and Asuka both go boso and hammer Zer. Bardiel swoops down to save his handsome big bro, and infects Eva-04 with Toji inside. Shinji and the new girl struggle to save Toji while putting that freak Bardiel out of action, while angry Asuka and Rei in her damaged Eva must struggle to stop Zeruel from getting into Central Dogma. The fight ends with both Sho and San having their dummy systems activated, and Yon is ripped apart. The new girl, who had a crush on Toji, screams in horror as Toji's entry plug is crushed(well...huh). Autopilot Sho goes genuinely boso and turns Zeruel into dinner once again, breaking free from its restraints and roaring. Seele shake their heads in dismay. Shinji and new girl rebel against Gendo for ordering Toji's death. Gogoki arrives from Germany, with Kaworu as the pilot, and he soothes quite a few upset nerves.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Sep 02, 2007 10:27 pm a female Shinji with some Asuka traits (Sailor Star Dust, rejoice!)

[]: Post by Reichu » Sun Sep 02, 2007 10:39 pm Rampant Speculation is rampaaaaaaant? Sounds like you need to write some fanfiction. Just let it alllll ouuuuuuut. How is anyone going to get an S2 Engine from an Angel, considering they burst like giant tomatoes the moment they die or something?

[]: Post by CorporalChaos » Sun Sep 02, 2007 10:41 pm Reichu wrote: Sounds like you need to write some fanfiction. Just let it alllll ouuuuuuut.

[]: Post by Hunter21 » Mon Sep 03, 2007 6:56 am Reichu wrote: How is anyone going to get an S2 Engine from an Angel, considering they burst like giant tomatoes the moment they die or something?

[]: Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Sep 03, 2007 8:14 am That whole reply was actually a big fat joke. I can't imagine five Evas ever coming together, and I have no idea whether that freak Bardiel is really returning or if the Wikipedia entry is wrong. I really hope Eva-03 gets a better deal than being mutated and then pulped. And that's not half as rampant as the stuff I've thrown at the Halo Story Page or the Halo Bungie Org forums (I was a contributor there under the same name). This situation is at least mildly feasible.

[]: Post by Morzella » Mon Sep 03, 2007 1:10 pm If Kaworu might have memorys from when this happned before perhaps the other angles do to. Most of them are optimists so have another go. However Zerule probaly had the worst time of it on Earth. in this movie ve will simpily say "You think I'm going through that agian? Bog off."

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Sep 03, 2007 2:51 pm The cyclical history concept is kind of stupid. I really hope the bloody seas and Moon are just from Adam exploding into blood at 2I or something. And that the "No 3, you really haven't changed" is not referring to the "Third Boy" (Shinji), but the Third Angel they shoved everyone up a notch to include (the giant hanging out with Kaworu?), or someone else.

[]: Post by Sandoz » Mon Sep 03, 2007 5:24 pm Is the Second Impact cover-up mentioned in Rebuild 01? A giant bloodstain on the moon would be difficult to blame on a meteor.

[]: Post by zeaven » Mon Sep 03, 2007 6:47 pm Sandoz wrote: Is the Second Impact cover-up mentioned in Rebuild 01? A giant bloodstain on the moon would be difficult to blame on a meteor.

[]: Post by CorporalChaos » Mon Sep 03, 2007 7:40 pm Sandoz wrote: Is the Second Impact cover-up mentioned in Rebuild 01? A giant bloodstain on the moon would be difficult to blame on a meteor.

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Sep 03, 2007 7:42 pm Sandoz wrote: Is the Second Impact cover-up mentioned in Rebuild 01? A giant bloodstain on the moon would be difficult to blame on a meteor.
